data.output:
1	---
2	title: Features
3	description: "DDx platform capabilities — stable, beta, and planned."
4	---
5	
6	DDx provides a focused set of platform primitives for AI-assisted development. Each capability is listed with its current maturity level.
7	
8	## Core Platform
9	
10	### Bead Tracker {{< maturity "stable" >}}
11	
12	Beads are the atomic unit of work in DDx. Each bead carries a title, description, acceptance criteria, dependency graph, and full state history. The `ddx bead` subcommands cover the complete CRUD lifecycle.
13	
14	```
15	ddx bead create "Add login endpoint" --ac "returns 200 on valid credentials"
16	ddx bead list --status ready
17	ddx bead close <id>
18	```
19	
20	### Agent Dispatch {{< maturity "stable" >}}
21	
22	Invoke AI agents through a single interface. DDx selects the configured harness, passes the prompt and context, and records token usage and cost per run.
23	
24	```
25	ddx agent run --harness claude --prompt prompts/implement.md
26	ddx agent execute-bead <id>
27	```
28	
29	### Execution Engine {{< maturity "stable" >}}
30	
31	Each bead executes in an isolated git worktree. Evidence is captured after every run. Passing beads are merged back; timed-out or failing runs are preserved for inspection.
32	
33	```
34	ddx agent execute-loop          # drain the queue
35	ddx agent execute-bead <id> --from HEAD
36	```
37	
38	### Plugin System {{< maturity "stable" >}}
39	
40	Workflow methodologies are packaged as plugins. One command installs a plugin under the project root — no global state, no machine-wide side effects.
41	
42	```
43	ddx install helix               # install HELIX workflow
44	ddx install ddx                 # update DDx default resources
45	```
46	
47	### Document Library {{< maturity "stable" >}}
48	
49	Prompts, patterns, templates, and personas live in versioned library directories. The library ships with DDx and is extended by plugins.
50	
51	```
52	ddx prompts list
53	ddx templates apply scaffold
54	ddx persona list
55	```
56	
57	### Persona System {{< maturity "stable" >}}
58	
59	Personas are reusable AI personality templates — code-reviewer, test-engineer, architect. Projects bind personas to roles in `.ddx.yml`. Every agent run picks up the bound persona automatically.
60	
61	```
62	ddx persona show code-reviewer
63	ddx persona bind code-reviewer --role reviewer
64	```
65	
66	### Git Sync {{< maturity "stable" >}}
67	
68	DDx resources are versioned via git subtree. `ddx update` pulls the latest library from the master repository; `ddx contribute` pushes improvements back.
69	
70	```
71	ddx update
72	ddx contribute
73	```
74	
75	## Extended Capabilities
76	
77	### MCP Server {{< maturity "beta" >}}
78	
79	DDx can expose beads, documents, and execution history over the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and HTTP. Remote supervisors — including Claude Desktop and compatible clients — can query work state and steer execution.
80	
81	```
82	ddx server start
83	```
84	
85	### Web UI {{< maturity "beta" >}}
86	
87	A browser interface for the bead tracker and document library. Built with SvelteKit and embedded in the `ddx-server` binary. Useful for reviewing bead state, execution history, and document relationships without leaving the browser.
88	
89	### Cost-Tiered Routing {{< maturity "beta" >}}
90	
91	Configure multiple model endpoints with tier labels. DDx routes beads to the cheapest model that meets the complexity threshold, escalating on failure. Optimize closed-work-per-dollar across Claude, Codex, Gemini, and local models.
92	
93	## Planned
94	
95	### Remote Execution {{< maturity "planned" >}}
96	
97	Run beads on remote compute — a cloud VM, a CI runner, or a dedicated agent host — without changing the local workflow. The bead queue and merge result flow stay identical.
98	
99	### Team Sync {{< maturity "planned" >}}
100	
101	Shared bead queues across a team. Agents on multiple machines claim, execute, and close beads from the same queue without coordination overhead.
102

Author: Test User <test@example.com>
Date:   Fri May 1 00:22:08 2026 -0400

    chore: fold refined plan into beads — primitives framing, sequencing deps, demo bead, features audit
    
    - /features/: single landing with anchors, artifact graph language, persona system removal
    - /why/: prerequisite for nav + homepage; four primitives scoped to DDx only
    - homepage: bridge copy requirement, demo path uses demos/ not demo/
    - nav: Reference locked (not Docs & Reference), depends on /why/ existing
    - new: demo recording bead (ddx-38d773c1), features audit bead (ddx-7880c7ae)
    - sequencing deps: nav + homepage blocked on /why/

","closing_commit_sha":"87472cfc708148de7a9b1c3e51383cd366f7fd94","created_at":"2026-04-14T18:35:26.618169063Z","description":"Rewrite the DDx admin UI from React+REST to SvelteKit+GraphQL in 4 stages.

Locked stack: SvelteKit (Svelte 5 runes, adapter-static, Bun package manager), Go gqlgen for GraphQL backend, Houdini for typed client, bits-ui + lucide-svelte + Tailwind for UI primitives, graphql-ws for subscriptions.

Constraints: existing REST endpoints stay frozen (CLI/MCP unchanged); existing isTrusted() localhost auth stays; delete React entirely and rebuild from specs (no port); URL scheme /nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/* from day one.

Full plan: docs/plan-svelte-migration.md

{"acceptance":"1) TestExecuteBeadOrphanRecovery no longer references fakeExecuteBeadGit or fakeAgentRunner. 2) Test dispatches through RealLandingGitOps + newScriptHarnessRepo (or equivalent real-git harness). 3) cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadOrphanRecovery$' -count=1 green. 4) No other test in agent_execute_bead_test.go is modified.","claimed-at":"2026-04-24T04:36:17Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"196235","closing_commit_sha":"6effebaea570aae432cb01e7d4456d56132e045c","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:24:42.174990312Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-02e5e3ba","depends_on_id":"ddx-915240dd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:42:24Z"}],"description":"Migrate cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go TestExecuteBeadOrphanRecovery() from fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner to real git + script harness.

Pattern:
- Use cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go newScriptHarnessRepo(t, beadCount) to build an isolated repo with real git.
- Use runGitInteg (from cli/internal/git/git_basic_test.go) for any supplemental git invocations, so GIT_* env is scrubbed.
- Replace fakeExecuteBeadGit with the RealLandingGitOps flow used in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go.
- Replace fakeAgentRunner with the script harness (cli/internal/agent/script.go) driven by a per-attempt directive file. When the test needs to simulate 'agent emits no commits' or 'agent emits a dirty worktree', encode that as script directives rather than a canned Result struct.
- Preserve every assertion — the point is that the test now exercises real git + real merge/preserve semantics, not that the surface shrinks.

Scope limit: ONLY this test. Do not migrate any other test in the same file; touching another test expands blast radius outside the bead.

Verification: cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadOrphanRecovery$' -count=1 passes. No struct ending in -GitOps or -Runner that fakes the thing under test is referenced from this test after the migration.

Governing concern: concerns.md §testing 'no mocks, period' + 'never mock the thing you are testing'. {"acceptance":"ddx agent execute-loop --harness claude --model claude-sonnet-4-6 invokes the claude binary, not ddx-agent; session log shows model: claude-sonnet-4-6 not model: vidar or model: bragi; if --model specifies an unrecognized claude model, the error is surfaced before any bead is claimed; ddx agent execute-loop --harness agent --model vidar continues to work as before (regression guard)","claimed-at":"2026-04-14T07:45:16Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1412283","closing_commit_sha":"e0f5fca79640c9ba54c8ef27113597e7e4dfaeaa","created_at":"2026-04-13T20:50:15.374772587Z","description":"When execute-loop is invoked with both --harness claude and --model <provider-name> (e.g. --model claude-sonnet-4-6), the --harness flag is silently ignored and the embedded agent harness is used instead, routing to whichever LM Studio provider is configured as default.

Observed: started execute-loop --local --harness claude --model claude-sonnet-4-6. Every session in the output showed 'model: vidar' or 'model: bragi' and all LLM responses were from qwen3.5-27b. No claude traffic was generated. The claude binary is healthy (ddx agent doctor reports OK). When execute-loop is started with --harness claude and NO --model flag, the claude binary is correctly invoked (confirmed by claude --print process appearing in ps output).

Root cause hypothesis: the --model flag value is being interpreted as a provider/route key by the embedded agent harness dispatch layer. When the model string matches a known provider name or a catalog entry that maps to the embedded-openai surface, the harness selection silently switches to 'agent' regardless of what --harness specifies. The --harness flag loses the race.

Required fix: --harness must take strict precedence over any routing implied by --model. If --harness claude is specified and the model string is not a valid claude model ID, the loop should either (a) pass the model string through to the claude binary as-is and let it fail with a clear error, or (b) emit a startup warning 'model X is not a recognized claude model; ignoring --model flag' and proceed with the harness default. Silent rerouting to a different harness is never acceptable.

Additionally: when the harness override causes a routing mismatch, the error or warning must appear before any bead is claimed, not after execution has started.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"failed to read worktree HEAD: git rev-parse HEAD: exit status 128","created_at":"2026-04-13T22:12:05.258556042Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"execution_failed"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"success

for line in sys.stdin:
    try:
        d = json.loads(line)
        if d.get('id') == 'ddx-4b202bbb':
            print('TITLE:', d.get('title'))
            print('DESC:', d.get('description',''))
            print('AC:', d.get('acceptance',''))
            break
    except: pass
"

input.old_string:
### Plugin System {{< maturity "stable" >}}

Workflow methodologies are packaged as plugins. One command installs a plugin under the project root — no global state, no machine-wide side effects.

```
ddx install helix               # install HELIX workflow
ddx install ddx                 # update DDx default resources
```

### Document Library {{< maturity "stable" >}}

Prompts, patterns, templates, and personas live in versioned library directories. The library ships with DDx and is extended by plugins.

```
ddx prompts list
ddx templates apply scaffold
ddx persona list
```

### Persona System {{< maturity "stable" >}}

Personas are reusable AI personality templates — code-reviewer, test-engineer, architect. Projects bind personas to roles in `.ddx.yml`. Every agent run picks up the bound persona automatically.

```
ddx persona show code-reviewer
ddx persona bind code-reviewer --role reviewer
```

### Git Sync {{< maturity "stable" >}}
input.new_string:
### Plugin System {{< maturity "stable" >}}

Plugins package additional resources — templates, prompts, patterns, personas, workflows. One command installs a plugin under the project root — no global state, no machine-wide side effects.

```
ddx install <plugin-name>       # install a plugin
ddx install ddx                 # update DDx default resources
```

### Document Library {{< maturity "stable" >}}

Prompts, patterns, templates, and personas live in versioned library directories. The library ships with DDx and is extended by plugins.

```
ddx prompts list
ddx templates apply scaffold
ddx persona list
```

### Artifact Graph {{< maturity "stable" >}}

DDx tracks the relationships between project artifacts — both markdown documents and non-markdown files. Each non-markdown artifact carries a `.ddx.yaml` sidecar that records its kind, provenance, and links to other artifacts, so the graph stays consistent across formats.

```
ddx graph show
ddx graph validate
```

### Git Sync {{< maturity "stable" >}}

data.output: (Bash completed with no output)

1	---
2	title: Features
3	description: "DDx platform capabilities — stable, beta, and planned."
4	---
5	
6	DDx provides a focused set of platform primitives for AI-assisted development. Each capability is listed with its current maturity level.
7	
8	## Core Platform
9	
10	### Bead Tracker {{< maturity "stable" >}}
11	
12	Beads are the atomic unit of work in DDx. Each bead carries a title, description, acceptance criteria, dependency graph, and full state history. The `ddx bead` subcommands cover the complete CRUD lifecycle.
13	
14	```
15	ddx bead create "Add login endpoint" --ac "returns 200 on valid credentials"
16	ddx bead list --status ready
17	ddx bead close <id>
18	```
19	
20	### Agent Dispatch {{< maturity "stable" >}}
21	
22	Invoke AI agents through a single interface. DDx selects the configured harness, passes the prompt and context, and records token usage and cost per run.
23	
24	```
25	ddx agent run --harness claude --prompt prompts/implement.md
26	ddx agent execute-bead <id>
27	```
28	
29	### Execution Engine {{< maturity "stable" >}}
30	
31	Each bead executes in an isolated git worktree. Evidence is captured after every run. Passing beads are merged back; timed-out or failing runs are preserved for inspection.
32	
33	```
34	ddx agent execute-loop          # drain the queue
35	ddx agent execute-bead <id> --from HEAD
36	```
37	
38	### Plugin System {{< maturity "stable" >}}
39	
40	Plugins package additional resources — templates, prompts, patterns, personas, workflows. One command installs a plugin under the project root — no global state, no machine-wide side effects.
41	
42	```
43	ddx install <plugin-name>       # install a plugin
44	ddx install ddx                 # update DDx default resources
45	```
46	
47	### Document Library {{< maturity "stable" >}}
48	
49	Prompts, patterns, templates, and personas live in versioned library directories. The library ships with DDx and is extended by plugins.
50	
51	```
52	ddx prompts list
53	ddx templates apply scaffold
54	ddx persona list
55	```
56	
57	### Artifact Graph {{< maturity "stable" >}}
58	
59	DDx tracks the relationships between project artifacts — both markdown documents and non-markdown files. Each non-markdown artifact carries a `.ddx.yaml` sidecar that records its kind, provenance, and links to other artifacts, so the graph stays consistent across formats.
60	
61	```
62	ddx doc graph
63	ddx doc audit
64	```
65	
66	### Git Sync {{< maturity "stable" >}}
67	
68	DDx resources are versioned via git subtree. `ddx update` pulls the latest library from the master repository; `ddx contribute` pushes improvements back.
69	
70	```
71	ddx update
72	ddx contribute
73	```
74	
75	## Extended Capabilities
76	
77	### MCP Server {{< maturity "beta" >}}
78	
79	DDx can expose beads, documents, and execution history over the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and HTTP. Remote supervisors — including Claude Desktop and compatible clients — can query work state and steer execution.
80	
81	```
82	ddx server start
83	```
84	
85	### Web UI {{< maturity "beta" >}}
86	
87	A browser interface for the bead tracker and document library. Built with SvelteKit and embedded in the `ddx-server` binary. Useful for reviewing bead state, execution history, and document relationships without leaving the browser.
88	
89	### Cost-Tiered Routing {{< maturity "beta" >}}
90	
91	Configure multiple model endpoints with tier labels. DDx routes beads to the cheapest model that meets the complexity threshold, escalating on failure. Optimize closed-work-per-dollar across Claude, Codex, Gemini, and local models.
92	
93	## Planned
94	
95	### Remote Execution {{< maturity "planned" >}}
96	
97	Run beads on remote compute — a cloud VM, a CI runner, or a dedicated agent host — without changing the local workflow. The bead queue and merge result flow stay identical.
98	
99	### Team Sync {{< maturity "planned" >}}
100	
101	Shared bead queues across a team. Agents on multiple machines claim, execute, and close beads from the same queue without coordination overhead.
102

data.text:
Committed. AC verified:
- Persona System section removed
- New Artifact Graph section describes documents and non-markdown files via `.ddx.yaml` sidecar
- No `ddx try` or `ddx run` (bare) references; `ddx agent run` and `ddx server start` are subcommands, not the user-facing `ddx run`
- HELIX/helix references removed from Plugin System
